2606.21071.title: Local LLM Agents as Vulnerable Runtimes:A Source-Code Audit of the Agent Runtime Layer
2606.21071.paper_summary: Local LLM agents such as OpenClaw and Nanobot run on end-user machines and act on host resources - the shell, filesystem, browser, stored credentials, and messaging applications - through natural-language goals. These agents have become privileged software runtimes that mediate between user intent, model outputs, and host-level actions. Existing research characterizes the landscape through prompt injection, malicious skills, marketplace risks, or black-box evaluation of agents. But the implementation layer that performs this mediation, the prompt builder, parser, tool dispatcher, skill loader, memory writer, network client, and permission gate, has remained an unexamined safety boundary. To our knowledge, no prior work has examined the agent's source tree to audit these components for implementation-level security weaknesses. We present CLAWAUDIT, a static auditing framework for measuring vulnerability exposure in local LLM agent runtimes. CLAWAUDIT derives a five-category vulnerability taxonomy from STRIDE and develops custom static-analysis rules that target agent-specific patterns absent from established rule sets for vulnerability analysis. We instantiate the taxonomy in two backends, 47 Semgrep YAML rules and 30 CodeQL queries, and evaluate on OPENCLAWBENCH, a benchmark of 446 source-code-level advisories from the OpenClaw repository and split temporally into 229 rule-derivation (train) and 217 held-out (test) advisories. On the held-out test, CLAWAUDIT raises Semgrep recall from 21.7% (Pro baseline) to 66.8%, and CodeQL recall from 13.8% (security-extended) to 75.1%. Train/test gaps remain within 4 percentage points for all four configurations, indicating that the rules generalize to vulnerabilities unseen during rule writing. A preliminary live-code audit shows that these recall-oriented rules require manual triage, motivating semantic filtering before production deployment.
2606.21071.paper_summary_zh: 當地的 LLM 代理，例如 OpenClaw 和 Nanobot，運行在終端用戶的機器上，並通過自然語言目標作用於主機資源——外殼、檔案系統、瀏覽器、儲存的憑證和消息應用程序。這些代理已成為特權軟體運行時，調解用戶意圖、模型輸出和主機級行動之間的關係。現有研究通過提示注入、惡意技能、市場風險或代理的黑箱評估來描述這一領域。但執行這一調解的實施層，即提示生成器、解析器、工具調度器、技能加載器、記憶寫入器、網絡客戶端和權限閘，仍然是一個未經檢查的安全邊界。據我們所知，之前的工作尚未檢查代理的源樹，以審計這些組件的實施級安全弱點。我們提出了 CLAWAUDIT，一個靜態審計框架，用於測量本地 LLM 代理運行時的漏洞暴露。CLAWAUDIT 從 STRIDE 衍生出五類漏洞分類法，並開發針對代理特定模式的自定義靜態分析規則，這些模式在現有的漏洞分析規則集中缺失。我們在兩個後端中實例化了這一分類法，47 個 Semgrep YAML 規則和 30 個 CodeQL 查詢，並在 OPENCLAWBENCH 上進行評估，該基準來自 OpenClaw 存儲庫的 446 個源代碼級建議，並在時間上分為 229 個規則推導（訓練）和 217 個保留（測試）建議。在保留測試中，CLAWAUDIT 將 Semgrep 的召回率從 21.7%（Pro 基準）提高到 66.8%，將 CodeQL 的召回率從 13.8%（安全擴展）提高到 75.1%。所有四種配置的訓練/測試差距保持在 4 個百分點以內，這表明這些規則可以推廣到在規則編寫期間未見的漏洞。一項初步的實時代碼審計顯示，這些以召回為導向的規則需要手動分流，這促使在生產部署之前進行語義過濾。
2606.21071.author: Zhengsong Zhang et.al.
2606.21071.authors: Zhengsong Zhang, Zongze Li, Jiawei Guo, Haipeng Cai
